title: Euclidean Algorithm
statement: For integers (a,b) not both zero, repeated division with remainder computes (\gcd(a,b)).
proof_structure:
* Show
  [\gcd(a,b)=\gcd(b,r)]
  because common divisors are preserved by (r=a-bq).
* Each remainder strictly decreases and stays nonnegative.
* Therefore process terminates.
* Final nonzero remainder is the greatest common divisor.

title: Binomial Theorem
statement: For any integer (n\ge 0),
proof_structure:
* Expand ((x+y)(x+y)\cdots(x+y)) with (n) factors.
* To get a term (x^{n-k}y^k), choose (k) of the (n) factors to contribute (y), the rest contribute (x).
* Number of such choices is [\binom{n}{k}.]
(Alternative proof: induction using Pascal identity.)

title: Triangle Inequality
statement: In any normed space,
proof_structure:
* Square both sides: [|a+b|^2=(a+b)^2=a^2+2ab+b^2.]
* Since [2ab\le 2|a||b|,] we get [|a+b|^2\le (|a|+|b|)^2.]
* Take square roots.
Use Cauchy–Schwarz on [|x+y|^2=|x|^2+2\Re\langle x,y\rangle+|y|^2.]

title: Infinitude of Primes
statement: There are infinitely many prime numbers.
proof_structure: Assume finitely many primes (p_1,\dots,p_n). Consider [N=p_1p_2\cdots p_n+1.] Then analyze prime divisors of (N).

title: Bézout’s Identity
statement: For integers (a,b), there exist integers (x,y) such that [ax+by=\gcd(a,b).]
proof_structure: Run the Euclidean Algorithm, then back-substitute each remainder equation upward until the gcd is written in terms of (a) and (b).

title: Cauchy–Schwarz Inequality
statement: In an inner product space, [|\langle x,y\rangle|\le |x||y|.]
proof_structure: Consider the nonnegative quadratic [|x-ty|^2\ge 0] and use its discriminant.

title: AM-GM Inequality
statement: For nonnegative real numbers (a_1,\dots,a_n), [\frac{a_1+\cdots+a_n}{n}\ge (a_1a_2\cdots a_n)^{1/n}.]
proof_structure: First prove the two-variable case using [(a-b)^2\ge 0.] Then extend to (n) variables by induction or repeated averaging.

title: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic
statement: Every integer (n>1) can be written as a product of primes, and this factorization is unique up to order.
proof_structure:
Use induction for existence. For uniqueness, if two prime factorizations exist, use Euclid’s lemma: a prime dividing a product must divide one factor.
Use Dirichlet characters and (L)-series; show (L(1,\chi)\neq 0) for nonprincipal characters, then relate to the distribution of primes via logarithmic divergence.

title: Chinese Remainder Theorem
statement: If (n_1,\dots,n_k) are pairwise coprime, then a system [x\equiv a_i \pmod{n_i}\quad (i=1,\dots,k)] has a unique solution modulo (N=n_1\cdots n_k).
proof_structure: For each (i), build a number that is (1) mod (n_i) and (0) mod all other (n_j), using Bézout coefficients. Then combine them linearly.

title: Fermat’s Little Theorem
statement: If (p) is prime and (p\nmid a), then [a^{p-1}\equiv 1 \pmod p.]
proof_structure: Multiply all nonzero residue classes mod (p): [1,2,\dots,p-1.] Then compare with their multiples by (a), which form the same set in a different order.

title: Euler’s Theorem
statement: If (\gcd(a,n)=1), then [a^{\varphi(n)}\equiv 1 \pmod n,] where (\varphi(n)) is the number of integers in ({1,\dots,n}) coprime to (n).
proof_structure: Look at the invertible residue classes modulo (n). Multiplication by (a) permutes them, just like in Fermat’s Little Theorem.

title: Lagrange’s Theorem (Group Theory)
statement: If (G) is a finite group and (H\le G) is a subgroup, then [|H| \mid |G|,\qquad |G|=[G:H]\cdot |H|.]
proof_structure: Partition (G) into left cosets of (H). Show every coset has exactly (|H|) elements.

title: Intermediate Value Theorem
statement: If (f:[a,b]\to\mathbb R) is continuous and (y) lies between (f(a)) and (f(b)), then there exists (c\in[a,b]) such that [f(c)=y.]
proof_structure: Consider all (x) where (f(x)<y), take the supremum of that set, then use continuity at the boundary point.

title: Rank–Nullity Theorem
statement: For a linear map (T:V\to W) between finite-dimensional vector spaces, [\dim(\ker T)+\dim(\operatorname{im}T)=\dim V.]
proof_structure: Take a basis of (\ker T), extend it to a basis of (V), then apply (T) to the added basis vectors.

title: Extreme Value Theorem
statement: If (f:[a,b]\to\mathbb R) is continuous, then (f) attains a maximum and a minimum on ([a,b]).
proof_structure: Continuous image of a compact set is compact; compact subsets of (\mathbb R) contain their supremum and infimum.

title: Rolle’s Theorem
statement: If (f) is continuous on ([a,b]), differentiable on ((a,b)), and [f(a)=f(b),] then there exists (c\in(a,b)) such that [f'(c)=0.]
proof_structure: By Extreme Value Theorem, (f) has max/min. If not constant, one extremum occurs inside ((a,b)); derivative at an interior extremum is zero.

title: Mean Value Theorem
statement: If (f) is continuous on ([a,b]) and differentiable on ((a,b)), then there exists (c\in(a,b)) such that [f'(c)=\frac{f(b)-f(a)}{b-a}.]
proof_structure: Subtract the secant line from (f) so the new function has equal endpoint values, then apply Rolle’s Theorem.

title: First Isomorphism Theorem
statement: For a homomorphism (\varphi:G\to H), [G/\ker\varphi \cong \operatorname{im}\varphi.]
proof_structure: Map each coset (g\ker\varphi) to (\varphi(g)). Then check well-definedness and bijectivity.

title: Gram–Schmidt Process
statement: Any linearly independent set ((v_1,\dots,v_n)) in an inner product space can be converted into an orthonormal set ((e_1,\dots,e_n)) spanning the same subspace.
proof_structure: Build vectors one at a time by subtracting projections onto previously constructed orthonormal vectors, then normalize.

title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
statement: If (f) is continuous and [F(x)=\int_a^x f(t)dt,] then [F'(x)=f(x).] Also, if (G'(x)=f(x)), then [\int_a^b f(x)dx=G(b)-G(a).]
proof_structure: Use a difference quotient for (F), then continuity of (f). For the second part, apply the Mean Value Theorem to (G).

title: Taylor’s Theorem
statement: If (f) is sufficiently differentiable near (a), then [f(x)=f(a)+f'(a)(x-a)+\frac{f''(a)}{2!}(x-a)^2+\cdots+\frac{f^{(n)}(a)}{n!}(x-a)^n+R_n(x),] where (R_n(x)) is a remainder term.
proof_structure: Subtract the Taylor polynomial from (f), then repeatedly apply Rolle’s Theorem / Mean Value Theorem to control the remainder.

title: Bolzano–Weierstrass Theorem
statement: Every bounded sequence in (\mathbb R^n) has a convergent subsequence.
proof_structure: Repeatedly bisect a containing interval/box and choose a nested sub-box containing infinitely many terms; then pick one term from each stage.

title: Gershgorin Circle Theorem
statement: Every eigenvalue of a complex matrix (A=(a_{ij})) lies in at least one disk [|z-a_{ii}|\le \sum_{j\ne i}|a_{ij}|.]
proof_structure: Take an eigenvector, choose a coordinate of maximal magnitude, and inspect the corresponding row of (Av=\lambda v).

title: Cauchy’s Theorem (Group Theory)
statement: If a prime (p) divides the order of a finite group (G), then (G) contains an element of order (p).
proof_structure: Let a cyclic group of order (p) act on suitable (p)-tuples / subsets of (G), then count fixed points modulo (p).

title: Parseval’s Identity
statement: If ({e_n}) is an orthonormal basis of a Hilbert space, then for any vector (x), [|x|^2=\sum_n |\langle x,e_n\rangle|^2.]
proof_structure: Use Pythagoras on finite partial sums of the orthogonal expansion, then pass to the limit using completeness.

title: Heine–Borel Theorem
statement: A subset of (\mathbb R^n) is compact iff it is closed and bounded.
proof_structure: Use Bolzano–Weierstrass / sequential compactness for one direction, and use open-cover arguments plus completeness for the other.

title: Banach Fixed Point Theorem
statement: If ((X,d)) is a complete metric space and (T:X\to X) satisfies [d(Tx,Ty)\le c d(x,y), \quad 0<c<1,] then (T) has a unique fixed point.
proof_structure: Iterate (x_{n+1}=T(x_n)); distances shrink geometrically, so the sequence is Cauchy and converges to the fixed point.

title: Liouville’s Theorem
statement: Every bounded entire function is constant.
proof_structure: Use Cauchy’s integral formula (or Cauchy estimates) to show all derivatives are zero.

title: Jordan–Hölder Theorem
statement: Any two composition series of a finite group have the same length, and the same simple factor groups up to order and isomorphism.
proof_structure: Refine two normal series to a common refinement, then compare successive quotient factors.

title: Fundamental Theorem of Symmetric Polynomials
statement: Every symmetric polynomial in (x_1,\dots,x_n) can be written as a polynomial in the elementary symmetric polynomials.
proof_structure: Order monomials, eliminate the leading symmetric monomial using suitable products of elementary symmetric polynomials, then iterate.

title: Spectral Theorem (Finite-Dimensional)
statement: A complex matrix (A) is normal iff there exists a unitary matrix (U) such that [U^*AU=D] with (D) diagonal.
proof_structure: First use Schur triangularization. Then show a normal upper-triangular matrix must already be diagonal.

title: Schur Triangularization Theorem
statement: Every complex square matrix (A) is unitarily similar to an upper triangular matrix: [U^*AU=T] for some unitary (U) and upper triangular (T).
proof_structure: Use existence of one eigenvector, extend it to an orthonormal basis, obtain a block upper-triangular form, then induct on dimension.

title: Singular Value Decomposition (SVD)
statement: For any matrix (A\in \mathbb C^{m\times n}), there exist unitary matrices (U,V) such that [A=U\Sigma V^*] where (\Sigma) is diagonal with nonnegative real entries.
proof_structure: Diagonalize (A^*A) using the Spectral Theorem; square roots of its eigenvalues become singular values.

title: Cauchy Integral Formula
statement: If (f) is holomorphic on and inside a simple closed curve (\gamma), and (a) lies inside, then [f(a)=\frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint_\gamma \frac{f(z)}{z-a}dz.]
proof_structure: Split [\frac{f(z)}{z-a} = \frac{f(z)-f(a)}{z-a}+\frac{f(a)}{z-a},} then apply Cauchy’s theorem to the first term.

title: Maximum Modulus Principle
statement: A nonconstant holomorphic function cannot attain a local maximum of (|f(z)|) at an interior point of its domain.
proof_structure: Use Cauchy Integral Formula / mean-value property: if (|f|) is maximal at the center, then boundary values force (f) to be constant nearby.

title: Schwarz Lemma
statement: If (f:\mathbb D\to\mathbb D) is holomorphic and (f(0)=0), then [|f(z)|\le |z|,\qquad |f'(0)|\le 1,] with equality cases giving rotations.
proof_structure: Consider [g(z)=\frac{f(z)}{z}] (with removable singularity at (0)), then apply the Maximum Modulus Principle.

title: Open Mapping Theorem (Complex Analysis)
statement: A nonconstant holomorphic function maps open sets to open sets.
proof_structure: Near (z_0), write [f(z)-f(z_0)=(z-z_0)^k g(z)] with (g(z_0)\neq 0). Then small circles around (z_0) map around (f(z_0)), forcing nearby values to be attained.

title: Residue Theorem
statement: If (f) is meromorphic inside a closed contour (\gamma), then [\oint_\gamma f(z)dz = 2\pi i \sum \operatorname{Res}(f;a_k),] where the sum is over enclosed poles.
proof_structure: Remove small circles around each pole, apply Cauchy Integral Theorem to the remaining region, and use Laurent coefficients on the small circles.

title: Argument Principle
statement: If (f) is meromorphic and has no zeros or poles on a closed contour (\gamma), then [\frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint_\gamma \frac{f'(z)}{f(z)}dz = N-P,] where (N) and (P) are the numbers of zeros and poles inside (\gamma), counted with multiplicity.
proof_structure: Near a zero/pole, write (f(z)=(z-a)^m g(z)), so (f'/f) has a simple pole with residue (m) (or (-m)).

title: Rouché’s Theorem
statement: If (f) and (g) are holomorphic inside/on a closed contour (\gamma), and [|g(z)|<|f(z)| \quad \text{on } \gamma,] then (f) and (f+g) have the same number of zeros inside (\gamma), counting multiplicity.
proof_structure: Interpolate via (f+t g) for (t\in[0,1]); boundary nonvanishing keeps the winding number / zero count unchanged.

title: Structure Theorem for Finite Abelian Groups
statement: Every finite abelian group is isomorphic to a direct product of cyclic groups of prime-power order.
proof_structure: Split the group into its (p)-primary parts, then repeatedly choose elements of maximal order and induct.

title: Gauss’s Lemma (Polynomial Version)
statement: The product of two primitive polynomials in (\mathbb Z[x]) is primitive.
proof_structure: Assume a prime divides every coefficient of the product. Reduce coefficients modulo that prime and derive a contradiction from degree behavior.

title: Eisenstein Criterion
statement:
If a polynomial [f(x)=a_nx^n+\cdots+a_0 \in \mathbb Z[x]] has a prime (p) such that:
* (p\nmid a_n)
* (p\mid a_0,\dots,a_{n-1})
* (p^2\nmid a_0)
then (f(x)) is irreducible over (\mathbb Q).
proof_structure: Assume a factorization into primitive integer polynomials and compare divisibility of constant terms and higher coefficients modulo (p).

title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
statement: If (0 \le f_n \uparrow f) pointwise (measurable functions), then [\int f_n \to \int f.]
proof_structure: Approximate (f) from below by simple functions; eventually those approximations are bounded by some (f_n).

title: Fatou’s Lemma
statement: For nonnegative measurable functions (f_n), [\int \liminf_{n\to\infty} f_n \le \liminf_{n\to\infty} \int f_n.]
proof_structure: Define [g_n=\inf_{k\ge n} f_k.] Then (g_n\uparrow \liminf f_n), so apply Monotone Convergence.

title: Riesz Representation Theorem (Hilbert Space Version)
statement: Every bounded linear functional (\phi) on a Hilbert space (H) can be written uniquely as [\phi(x)=\langle x,y\rangle] for some unique (y\in H).
proof_structure: Look at (\ker \phi), take a vector orthogonal to it, and decompose every vector into kernel part plus one orthogonal direction.

title: Dominated Convergence Theorem
statement: If (f_n \to f) almost everywhere and [|f_n| \le g] for some integrable (g), then [\int f_n \to \int f.]
proof_structure: Use Fatou’s Lemma on (g \pm f_n), or apply Fatou to (2g-|f_n-f|).

title: Fredholm Alternative (finite-dimensional form)
statement: For a matrix (A), [Ax=b] is solvable iff (b\perp \ker(A^*)).
proof_structure: Use orthogonal complements and dimension arguments: vectors orthogonal to all columns are exactly (\ker(A^*)).

title: Arzelà–Ascoli Theorem
statement: A uniformly bounded equicontinuous family of functions on a compact set has a uniformly convergent subsequence.
proof_structure: Choose a countable dense set, extract a diagonal subsequence converging there, then use equicontinuity to upgrade pointwise convergence to uniform convergence.

title: Cayley–Hamilton Theorem
statement: Every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic polynomial: [p_A(A)=0.]
proof_structure: Use the adjugate identity [(\lambda I-A)\operatorname{adj}(\lambda I-A)=p_A(\lambda)I] and interpret it as a polynomial identity before substituting (\lambda=A).

title: Fubini’s Theorem
statement: If (f) is integrable on a product space (X\times Y), then [\int_{X\times Y} f(x,y)d(x,y) = \int_X\left(\int_Y f(x,y)dy\right)dx] (and similarly with the order reversed).
proof_structure: First prove it for indicator functions of rectangles, then simple functions, then extend by approximation to general integrable functions.

title: Law of Large Numbers
statement: For i.i.d. random variables (X_1,X_2,\dots) with mean (\mu), [\frac{X_1+\cdots+X_n}{n}\to \mu] (in probability or almost surely).
proof_structure: Control variance of averages (weak form), or combine truncation + Borel–Cantelli for almost sure convergence.

title: Uniform Boundedness Principle
statement: Let ({T_\alpha}) be bounded linear operators from a Banach space (X) to a normed space (Y). If for every (x\in X), [\sup_\alpha |T_\alpha x|<\infty,] then [\sup_\alpha |T_\alpha|<\infty.]
proof_structure: Consider sets where operators are uniformly bounded on a point. Use Baire Category Theorem to upgrade pointwise boundedness to norm boundedness.

title: Closed Graph Theorem
statement: If (T:X\to Y) is a linear operator between Banach spaces and its graph {(x,Tx):x\in X} is closed in (X\times Y), then (T) is bounded.
proof_structure: View the graph itself as a Banach space, then use projection maps together with the Open Mapping Theorem.

title: Open Mapping Theorem (Banach Spaces)
statement: If (T:X\to Y) is a surjective bounded linear operator between Banach spaces, then (T) maps open sets to open sets.
proof_structure: Show the image of the unit ball is dense enough to contain a small ball around (0), then use scaling and translation.

title: Hilbert Basis Theorem
statement: If (R) is a Noetherian ring, then the polynomial ring [R[x]] is also Noetherian.
proof_structure: For an ideal in (R[x]), look at leading coefficients of polynomials of each degree. Use finite generation in (R), then induct on degree.

title: Krull Intersection Theorem
statement: If (R) is Noetherian, (I\subseteq J(R)), and (M) is a finitely generated (R)-module, then [\bigcap_{n\ge1} I^n M = 0.]
proof_structure: Let [N=\bigcap I^nM.] Show (N=IN), then apply Nakayama’s Lemma.

title: Central Limit Theorem
statement: For i.i.d. random variables with mean (\mu) and variance (\sigma^2), [\frac{(X_1+\cdots+X_n)-n\mu}{\sigma\sqrt n} \Rightarrow N(0,1).]
proof_structure: Compute characteristic functions of normalized sums, use Taylor expansion near (0), then pass to the limit.

title: Perron–Frobenius Theorem
statement: A positive square matrix has a largest positive real eigenvalue with a positive eigenvector.
proof_structure: Map the positive simplex to itself using the matrix, then use compactness / fixed-point ideas; stronger versions handle irreducible nonnegative matrices.

title: Jordan Canonical Form
statement: Every complex square matrix is similar to a block diagonal matrix made of Jordan blocks.
proof_structure: Decompose the space into generalized eigenspaces, then for each eigenvalue analyze chains of vectors under ((A-\lambda I)).

title: Inverse Function Theorem
statement: If (f:\mathbb R^n\to\mathbb R^n) is continuously differentiable and the derivative (Df(a)) is invertible, then (f) is locally invertible near (a), with differentiable inverse.
proof_structure: Near (a), (f) is well-approximated by its invertible linearization; use a contraction-map argument or Newton iteration locally.

title: Implicit Function Theorem
statement: If (F(x,y)=0) and the derivative with respect to (y) is invertible at a point, then locally one can solve uniquely for [y=g(x).]
proof_structure: Apply the Inverse Function Theorem to the map [(x,y)\mapsto (x,F(x,y)).]

title: Hahn–Banach Theorem
statement: A bounded linear functional defined on a subspace can be extended to the whole vector space without increasing its norm.
proof_structure: Extend one dimension at a time while preserving bounds, then use Zorn’s Lemma to obtain a maximal extension.

title: Stone–Weierstrass Theorem
statement: If a subalgebra (A\subset C(K)) on a compact space (K) contains constants and separates points, then (A) is dense in (C(K)).
proof_structure: Use the algebra operations to approximate maxima/minima and then build global approximations from point-separating local ones.

title: Structure Theorem for PID Modules
statement: Every finitely generated module over a principal ideal domain is a direct sum of a free part and cyclic torsion modules.
proof_structure: Represent the module by generators/relations, then diagonalize the relation matrix using row/column operations (Smith normal form).

title: Wedderburn’s Little Theorem
statement: Every finite division ring is commutative.
proof_structure: Assume a smallest counterexample. Study conjugacy classes and proper subfields, then derive a counting contradiction.

title: Hilbert–Schmidt Theorem
statement: A compact self-adjoint operator on a Hilbert space has an orthonormal basis of eigenvectors.
proof_structure: Use the compact self-adjoint spectral theorem, then expand vectors in the orthonormal eigenbasis.

title: Compactness Theorem (First-Order Logic)
statement: A set of first-order sentences has a model iff every finite subset has a model.
proof_structure: If the whole set were inconsistent, some finite proof would use only finitely many axioms. Use completeness of first-order logic.

title: Schauder Fixed Point Theorem
statement: A continuous map from a nonempty compact convex subset of a Banach space to itself has a fixed point.
proof_structure: Approximate the compact set by finite-dimensional pieces, apply Brouwer Fixed Point Theorem there, then pass to a limit.

title: Nielsen–Schreier Theorem
statement: Every subgroup of a free group is itself free.
proof_structure: Choose coset representatives and explicitly construct generators (Schreier generators), or use covering spaces of graphs.

title: Noether Normalization Lemma
statement: Every finitely generated algebra over a field is integral over a polynomial ring in finitely many algebraically independent variables.
proof_structure: Change generators cleverly so one satisfies a monic polynomial relation, then induct on the number of generators.

title: Nullstellensatz (Hilbert’s Nullstellensatz)
statement: Over an algebraically closed field (k), maximal ideals of [k[x_1,\dots,x_n]] are exactly ideals of the form [(x_1-a_1,\dots,x_n-a_n).]
proof_structure: For a maximal ideal (M), study the field (k[x_1,\dots,x_n]/M); show it must be an algebraic extension of (k), hence equals (k).

title: Borel–Cantelli Lemmas
statement:
For events (A_1,A_2,\dots):
1. If [\sum_{n=1}^\infty P(A_n)<\infty,] then with probability (1), only finitely many (A_n) occur.
2. If the (A_n) are independent and [\sum_{n=1}^\infty P(A_n)=\infty,] then with probability (1), infinitely many occur.
proof_structure: Use subadditivity for the first part; for the second, use complements and independence.

title: Brouwer Fixed Point Theorem
statement: Every continuous map [f:B^n \to B^n] from the closed unit ball to itself has a fixed point.
proof_structure: Assume no fixed point. Then each point can be pushed radially to the boundary, producing a continuous retraction (B^n \to S^{n-1}), which is impossible.

title: Jordan Curve Theorem
statement: Every simple closed curve in the plane divides the plane into exactly two connected regions: an inside and an outside.
proof_structure: Define winding number for points off the curve, show it is locally constant, equals (0) outside, nonzero inside, and exclude extra components.

title: Tychonoff Theorem
statement: Any product of compact topological spaces is compact.
proof_structure: Use the finite intersection property / ultrafilters / nets coordinatewise, then assemble a point in the full product using Choice.

title: Gauss–Bonnet Theorem
statement: For a compact oriented surface (M), [\int_M K dA = 2\pi \chi(M),] where (K) is Gaussian curvature and (\chi(M)) is the Euler characteristic.
proof_structure: Triangulate the surface, relate angle defect in triangles to curvature, then sum globally.

title: Seifert–van Kampen Theorem
statement: If a topological space (X = U \cup V) with (U, V, U \cap V) path-connected, then [\pi_1(X) \cong \pi_1(U) *_{\pi_1(U\cap V)} \pi_1(V).]
proof_structure: Break loops into pieces lying in (U) or (V), translate concatenation of loops into group generators and relations.

title: Primary Decomposition Theorem
statement: In a Noetherian ring, every ideal can be written as an intersection of finitely many primary ideals.
proof_structure: Use Noetherian induction / maximal counterexample arguments, splitting ideals using zero divisors and associated primes.

title: Sard’s Theorem
statement: For a sufficiently smooth map (f:\mathbb R^n \to \mathbb R^m), the set of critical values has measure zero.
proof_structure: Near critical points, the map flattens directions; cover domain by small cubes and estimate that images of those cubes have very small total volume.

title: de Rham Theorem
statement: For a smooth manifold (M), de Rham cohomology is naturally isomorphic to singular cohomology with real coefficients: [H^k_{dR}(M)\cong H^k(M;\mathbb R).]
proof_structure: Integrate differential forms over simplices to build a cochain map, then prove it is an isomorphism using local exactness + Mayer–Vietoris patching.

title: Mercer’s Theorem
statement: A continuous symmetric positive kernel (K(x,y)) on a compact domain has an eigenfunction expansion [K(x,y)=\sum_{n=1}^\infty \lambda_n \phi_n(x)\phi_n(y).]
proof_structure: Convert the kernel into a compact self-adjoint integral operator, apply the spectral theorem, then use continuity to upgrade convergence.

title: Löwenheim–Skolem Theorem
statement: If a first-order theory has an infinite model, then it has models of other cardinalities (in particular, a countable model in the downward form).
proof_structure: Add Skolem functions and take the closure of a countable set under those functions (Skolem hull), producing a countable elementary submodel.

title: Prime Number Theorem
statement: If (\pi(x)) is the number of primes (\le x), then [\pi(x)\sim \frac{x}{\log x}.]
proof_structure: Study the Riemann zeta function (\zeta(s)), relate its logarithmic derivative to primes, prove suitable zero-free behavior near (\Re(s)=1), then use Tauberian/contour arguments.

title: Spectral Theorem (Compact Self-Adjoint Operators)
statement: A compact self-adjoint operator on a Hilbert space has an orthonormal basis of eigenvectors, with real eigenvalues accumulating only at (0).
proof_structure: Maximize the Rayleigh quotient to obtain one eigenvector, restrict to its orthogonal complement, then iterate using compactness.

title: Gelfand–Naimark Theorem
statement: Every commutative (C^*)-algebra with identity is isometrically isomorphic to the algebra of continuous functions on its space of maximal ideals.
proof_structure: Map elements to continuous functions via Gelfand transform (evaluation at characters), then use Stone–Weierstrass to show it is a surjective isometry.

title: Stone’s Theorem on One-Parameter Unitary Groups
statement: Every strongly continuous unitary group ((U_t)_{t\in\mathbb R}) on a Hilbert space has the form [U_t=e^{itA}] for a unique self-adjoint operator (A).
proof_structure: Use the spectral theorem to exponentiate a self-adjoint operator; conversely recover the generator from the strong derivative / resolvent.

title: Gödel’s First Incompleteness Theorem
statement: Any consistent effectively axiomatized theory strong enough to encode arithmetic contains a true arithmetic statement that it cannot prove.
proof_structure: Encode syntax as numbers (Gödel numbering), then diagonalize to construct a sentence that asserts its own unprovability.

title: Gödel’s Second Incompleteness Theorem
statement: Any consistent, effectively axiomatized theory strong enough for arithmetic cannot prove its own consistency.
proof_structure: Formalize the First Incompleteness Theorem inside the theory itself; if the theory proved its own consistency, it would prove its Gödel sentence, contradiction.

title: Kolmogorov Extension Theorem
statement: A consistent family of finite-dimensional probability distributions defines a probability measure on the infinite product space.
proof_structure: Define probabilities on cylinder sets, verify finite additivity/consistency, then extend using Carathéodory’s extension theorem.

title: Artin–Wedderburn Theorem
statement: Every semisimple Artinian ring is isomorphic to a finite product of matrix rings over division rings.
proof_structure: Decompose the regular module into simple modules; endomorphism rings of these pieces produce matrix blocks.

title: Hodge Decomposition Theorem
statement: On a compact Riemannian manifold, every differential form decomposes uniquely as [\omega = d\alpha + \delta\beta + h,] where (h) is harmonic.
proof_structure: Study the Laplacian [\Delta = d\delta + \delta d] as a self-adjoint elliptic operator, then use orthogonal decomposition of Hilbert spaces.

title: Riemann Mapping Theorem
statement: Every simply connected proper open subset of (\mathbb C) is conformally equivalent to the unit disk.
proof_structure: Construct a family of injective holomorphic maps into the disk, maximize derivative at a base point, then use normal families (Montel theorem) to extract a limit.

title: Brouwer Invariance of Domain
statement: If (U \subset \mathbb{R}^n) is open and (f:U \to \mathbb{R}^n) is continuous and injective, then (f(U)) is open and (f) is a homeomorphism onto its image.
proof_structure: Reduce to showing a non-degenerate continuous injective map cannot “collapse dimension”; use homology / degree theory or invariance of domain via Brouwer fixed-point ideas.

title: Radon–Nikodym Theorem
statement:
If (
u) is absolutely continuous with respect to (\mu) ((
u \ll \mu)), then there exists a measurable function (f) such that [
u(A)=\int_A f d\mu] for all measurable sets (A).
proof_structure: Work in (L^1(\mu)); construct (f) as a maximizer using approximation by simple functions or via Hilbert space methods.

title: Riemann–Roch Theorem
statement: For a divisor (D) on a compact Riemann surface, [\ell(D) - \ell(K-D) = \deg(D) + 1 - g,] where (\ell(D)) is the dimension of meromorphic functions with poles bounded by (D), (K) is the canonical divisor, and (g) is the genus.
proof_structure: Compare spaces of meromorphic functions and differentials; use residue pairing and count dimensions via cohomological arguments.

title: Whitehead Theorem
statement: A map (f: X \to Y) between connected CW-complexes is a homotopy equivalence if it induces an isomorphism on all homotopy groups (\pi_n).
proof_structure: Attach cells to (f) to make it an isomorphism on homology; show that (f) must be a homotopy equivalence using lifting properties.

title: Spectral Theorem (General Normal Operators)
statement: Every bounded normal operator (T) on a complex Hilbert space admits a spectral resolution [T=\int_{\sigma(T)} z dE(z),] where (E) is a projection-valued measure.
proof_structure: Build continuous functional calculus for normal operators, then extend from polynomials/continuous functions to measurable sets, producing spectral projections.

title: Stokes’ Theorem
statement: For a smooth oriented manifold (M) with boundary, [\int_M d\omega = \int_{\partial M} \omega,] where (\omega) is a differential form.
proof_structure: First prove in (\mathbb{R}^n) using repeated 1D Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, then extend via partitions of unity and coordinate charts.
